instructionsection.title: SDOT (2-way, multiple and indexed vector) -- A64
instructionsection.heading: SDOT (2-way, multiple and indexed vector)

brief.para: Multi-vector signed 16-bit integer dot product by indexed element to 32-bit integer

authored.para: This instruction computes the dot product of two signed 16-bit integer values held in each 32-bit element of the two or four first source vectors and two signed 16-bit integer values in the corresponding indexed 32-bit element of the second source vector. The widened dot product result is destructively added to the corresponding 32-bit element of the ZA single-vector groups.
The groups within the second source vector are specified using an immediate element index that selects the same group position within each 128-bit vector segment. The index range is from 0 to 3.
The single-vector group within each half of or each quarter of the ZA array is selected by the sum of the vector select register and offset, modulo half or quarter the number of ZA array vectors.

The vector group symbol, VGx2 or VGx4, indicates that the ZA operand consists of two or four ZA single-vector groups respectively. The vector group symbol is preferred for disassembly, but optional in assembler source code.

uses_dit.condition: FEAT_SVE2 is implemented or FEAT_SME is implemented

operationalnote_content.para: This instruction is a data-independent-time instruction as described in About PSTATE.DIT.

a.hover: Is the 32-bit name of the vector select register W8-W11, encoded in the "Rv" field.
Is the vector select offset, in the range 0 to 7, encoded in the "off3" field.
For the "Two ZA single-vectors" variant: is the name of the first scalable vector register of the first source multi-vector group, encoded as "Zn" times 2.
Is the name of the second scalable vector register of the first source multi-vector group, encoded as "Zn" times 2 plus 1.
For the "Double-precision" variant: is the name of the second source scalable vector register Z0-Z15, encoded in the "Zm" field.
Is the immediate index of a pair of 16-bit elements within each 128-bit vector segment, in the range 0 to 3, encoded in the "i2" field.

ps.name: A64.sme.mortlach_multi_indexed_2.mortlach_multi2_zza_idx_s.sdot_za32_zzi_2xi

a.hover: Is the 32-bit name of the vector select register W8-W11, encoded in the "Rv" field.
Is the vector select offset, in the range 0 to 7, encoded in the "off3" field.
For the "Four ZA single-vectors" variant: is the name of the first scalable vector register of the first source multi-vector group, encoded as "Zn" times 4.
Is the name of the fourth scalable vector register of the first source multi-vector group, encoded as "Zn" times 4 plus 3.
For the "Double-precision" variant: is the name of the second source scalable vector register Z0-Z15, encoded in the "Zm" field.
Is the immediate index of a pair of 16-bit elements within each 128-bit vector segment, in the range 0 to 3, encoded in the "i2" field.

ps.name: A64.sme.mortlach_multi_indexed_3.mortlach_multi4_zza_idx_s.sdot_za32_zzi_4xi

intro.para: Is the 32-bit name of the vector select register W8-W11, encoded in the "Rv" field.
Is the vector select offset, in the range 0 to 7, encoded in the "off3" field.
For the "Two ZA single-vectors" variant: is the name of the first scalable vector register of the first source multi-vector group, encoded as "Zn" times 2.
For the "Four ZA single-vectors" variant: is the name of the first scalable vector register of the first source multi-vector group, encoded as "Zn" times 4.
Is the name of the second scalable vector register of the first source multi-vector group, encoded as "Zn" times 2 plus 1.
Is the name of the second source scalable vector register Z0-Z15, encoded in the "Zm" field.
Is the immediate index of a pair of 16-bit elements within each 128-bit vector segment, in the range 0 to 3, encoded in the "i2" field.
Is the name of the fourth scalable vector register of the first source multi-vector group, encoded as "Zn" times 4 plus 3.

ps.name: A64.sme.mortlach_multi_indexed_2.mortlach_multi2_zza_idx_s.sdot_za32_zzi_2xi
